recipes.name: Aloo Gobi
recipes.description: A classic Indian dry curry made with potatoes and cauliflower, spiced with aromatic Indian spices.

steps.title: Prepare the Vegetables
steps.instruction: Wash and peel the potatoes, then cut them into medium-sized cubes. Break the cauliflower into bite-sized florets. Chop the onions finely, dice the tomatoes, mince the garlic, grate the ginger, and slit the green chilies.
steps.tip: Soak potato cubes in water for 10 minutes to remove excess starch for crispier texture.

steps.title: Heat Oil and Temper Spices
steps.instruction: Heat oil in a large kadai or wok on medium-high heat. Once hot, add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds. This releases their aroma into the oil, which is the base of the entire dish.
steps.tip: Oil must be hot enough. Test by dropping one cumin seed; it should sizzle immediately.

steps.title: Saute Onions, Garlic and Ginger
steps.instruction: Add sliced onions and saute on medium heat until golden brown, about 6 to 7 minutes. Then add minced garlic, grated ginger, and green chilies. Stir and cook for 2 more minutes until the raw smell disappears completely.
steps.tip: Do not rush the onion browning. This builds the flavor foundation of your curry.

steps.title: Add Tomatoes and Spices
steps.instruction: Add chopped tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, red chili powder, and salt. Mix well and cook on medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes until tomatoes soften completely and oil starts separating from the masala.
steps.tip: The oil separating is a sign your masala is perfectly cooked. Do not skip this step.

steps.title: Add Potatoes First
steps.instruction: Add the potato cubes first. Toss to coat them well with the masala. Cover and cook on medium-low heat for 8 minutes, stirring once halfway. Potatoes need a head start as they take longer to cook than cauliflower.
steps.tip: Keep flame on medium-low to avoid burning the bottom. Add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water if needed.

steps.title: Add Cauliflower
steps.instruction: Add cauliflower florets and gently toss everything together. Cover and cook for another 10 to 12 minutes on medium-low heat, stirring every 3 to 4 minutes. Both vegetables should be tender but not mushy.
steps.tip: Do not add water. Dry cooking gives Aloo Gobi its characteristic texture.

steps.title: Finish with Garam Masala
steps.instruction: Once both vegetables are cooked through, sprinkle garam masala over the dish. Toss gently and cook uncovered for 2 minutes on high heat to roast the garam masala and dry any remaining moisture.
steps.tip: Cooking garam masala at the end preserves its delicate aromatic oils.

steps.title: Garnish and Serve
steps.instruction: Turn off the heat. Garnish generously with freshly chopped coriander leaves. Your Aloo Gobi is ready. Serve hot with roti, paratha, or steamed rice. It also pairs beautifully with dal.
steps.tip: A squeeze of lemon juice just before serving brightens all the flavors.

recipes.name: Palak Paneer
recipes.description: A rich and creamy Indian dish of fresh spinach puree with soft paneer cubes, mildly spiced for a comforting meal.

steps.title: Blanch the Spinach
steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add spinach leaves and blanch for exactly 2 minutes. Immediately transfer to a bowl of ice-cold water to stop cooking and preserve the bright green color. Drain well after 2 minutes.
steps.tip: The ice bath is non-negotiable for that restaurant-quality vibrant green color.

steps.title: Blend Spinach Puree
steps.instruction: In a blender, combine the blanched spinach, green chilies, and half the ginger. Blend to a completely smooth, velvety puree. Do not add extra water as the residual moisture is enough. Set aside.
steps.tip: For extra smooth texture, blend twice and pass through a strainer for restaurant quality.

steps.title: Fry the Paneer
steps.instruction: Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil in a pan. Add paneer cubes and fry on medium-high heat for 2 to 3 minutes per side until golden. Remove and immediately place in warm water to keep them soft. This prevents rubberiness.
steps.tip: Soaking fried paneer in warm water for 5 minutes keeps it melt-in-mouth soft.

steps.title: Build the Masala Base
steps.instruction: In the same pan, heat remaining oil or ghee. Add bay leaf and cumin seeds. Once they splutter, add onions and cook on medium heat for 8 to 9 minutes until deep golden brown. Add remaining garlic and ginger paste, cook 2 minutes.
steps.tip: Deeply caramelized onions are what separate good palak paneer from great palak paneer.

steps.title: Add Tomato and Spices
steps.instruction: Add pureed tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook on medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring frequently, until the masala thickens and oil separates clearly on the sides.
steps.tip: The masala must be fully cooked. Raw tomato taste ruins the dish.

steps.title: Add Spinach Puree
steps.instruction: Reduce heat to low. Add the spinach puree to the masala. Stir everything together gently. Cook on low heat for 5 minutes. Do not boil vigorously as it discolors the spinach and destroys Vitamin C.
steps.tip: Keep the flame on low after adding spinach. High heat ruins the color and nutrients.

steps.title: Add Paneer and Cream
steps.instruction: Add the fried paneer cubes drained from water. Stir gently so paneer does not break. Add fresh cream and crushed kasuri methi. Stir carefully and cook for 3 minutes on low heat.
steps.tip: Crush kasuri methi between your palms before adding to release maximum flavor.

steps.title: Finish with Garam Masala
steps.instruction: Sprinkle garam masala over the dish. Stir gently and cook for 1 final minute. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Remove bay leaf. Your Palak Paneer is ready, rich, green, and aromatic.
steps.tip: A drizzle of cream on top right before serving makes it look and taste restaurant-grade.

recipes.name: Mixed Vegetable Stir Fry
recipes.description: A quick and healthy stir fry with colorful vegetables in a savory sauce.

steps.title: Prep All Vegetables
steps.instruction: Cut all vegetables uniformly. Julienne the carrots, slice bell peppers, break broccoli into small florets, shred cabbage, trim beans. Have everything ready before you start as stir fry moves fast.
steps.tip: Mise en place is critical for stir fry. Once the wok is hot, there is no time to chop.

steps.title: Make the Sauce
steps.instruction: Mix soy sauce and sesame oil in a small bowl. Whisk well and set aside. This sauce will coat the vegetables and create a beautiful glossy finish.
steps.tip: Adding a pinch of sugar balances the salt in the soy sauce.

steps.title: Heat the Wok
steps.instruction: Heat a wok or large pan on maximum heat for 2 minutes until smoking. Add vegetable oil and swirl to coat. The extreme heat gives stir fry its characteristic smoky and charred flavor.
steps.tip: Never stir fry in a cold wok. High heat is the soul of this technique.

steps.title: Stir Fry Aromatics
steps.instruction: Add garlic, ginger, and onion. Stir vigorously for 60 seconds on high heat. They should be fragrant and starting to color at the edges.
steps.tip: Keep stirring constantly. Nothing should sit still in a hot wok.

steps.title: Add Hard Vegetables
steps.instruction: Add carrots, broccoli, and green beans. Stir fry vigorously for 3 to 4 minutes on high heat. Add a splash of water if the pan gets too dry.
steps.tip: A splash of water will steam-cook the harder vegetables evenly.

steps.title: Add Soft Vegetables
steps.instruction: Add bell peppers and cabbage. Stir fry for 2 more minutes. These cook quickly and should retain a slight crunch.
steps.tip: Overcooked bell peppers lose their sweetness and vibrant color.

steps.title: Add Sauce and Finish
steps.instruction: Pour the sauce over the vegetables. Toss everything vigorously for 1 minute until the sauce coats every piece and turns glossy. Add black pepper and salt to taste. Serve immediately.
steps.tip: Stir fry waits for no one. Serve the moment it is done.
